title: Conditional Rendering
summary: Conditional rendering shows different things based on the data.
You use normal JavaScript logic inside your JSX.
This is how you show a loading message, then the content.
It keeps the screen matching the situation.

sections.text: A real screen is not always the same. It might show a spinner while loading, then the data, or a message if something is empty. Choosing what to show based on the current situation is called conditional rendering.

sections.title: Showing Based on a Condition
sections.text: Conditional rendering means deciding what to display using the data you have. If you are still loading, show a loading message. If there is no data, show an empty state. If you have data, show it. You use ordinary JavaScript logic to make these choices right inside your component.

sections.title: It Is Just JavaScript
sections.text: There is nothing special to learn here beyond the logic you already know. Because JSX is really JavaScript, you use the same if checks and simple expressions to pick what to return. This is why learning the fundamentals first pays off, you are just applying them inside a component.

media.alt: A component choosing between loading, empty, and data views

sections.title: A Sign That Changes
sections.text: Conditional rendering is like a shop sign that changes with the situation. Closed when the shop is shut, open when it is running, back in five minutes when on a break. The sign always matches the real state. Your component does the same, showing the right thing for the current data.

media.alt: A shop sign switching to match the current situation

sections.title: Rendering Conditionally
items: Look at the data the component has.
Decide what to show in each situation.
Use normal JavaScript logic to choose.
Return the right markup for that case.
As the data changes, the right view appears.

sections.text: A very common pattern is showing a loading message, then the content, then an error view if something failed. Those three states, loading, success, and error, cover most data driven screens.

terms.definition: Showing different things based on the data.
The check that decides what to show.
What you show while data is on its way.
What you show when there is no data.

options: Shows different things based on the data
questions.explanation: Conditional rendering shows different views depending on the current data.

questions.text: What do you use to choose what to show?
questions.explanation: You use ordinary JavaScript logic, since JSX is really JavaScript.

questions.text: What three states cover most data screens?
questions.explanation: Loading, success, and error cover most data driven screens.
